Hi everyone, I just want a help from you
.
I really like playing with saya and helot, but I  don't know if should play shield's or healing. 
.
This is all the characters that I have right now.
it really only matters for saya specifically, with her massive Aliemus costs, it lets her be a bit less bad at printing children and a bit more good at inflicting corrosion

I won't talk you out of playing saya with ghelot if you want to do it, but they do have an anti-synergy. Saya 0 cost, her strongest card by far, throws away your cards, which is exactly the opposite of what you want for ghelot
I don't mind changing my team if you have an idea in your mind
Gelot 4-cost (her main dmg btw) will be discarded when you use saya’s 1 cost corrosion

If you want to keep playing them together anyways, you might as well lean into the balls to the wall gameplay style of Saya and Ghelot and forfeit on any healers, instead opting for Aigis for vuln and keyflare, and some shielder, probably alva or Nautila for the chaos effect
I started a week ago not that experience 😅
Helot, Ghelot, Aigis, Leigh
should be pretty fun. Helot's only purpose here is to click ult button before you use Bloodthirsty Flail
but it's fine, her only purpose is the ult button in basically any comp featuring her
I will say though, that you'll want ghelot e2 at least, and should the opportunity present itself, to swap Leigh out for Thais
once/if you get her
So just keep getting her ult and use it before using her cards
also str bots since her 4 cost has 5x str multiplier (clem, horla, etc)
before Catena's cards
if you want to play Saya, there is Saya Lotan Ramona/Ogier/Aigis/whoever for absolutely-budget free-to-play team
That's kinda the only purpose for leigh here. There's not really a better strength bot available in the character pool, except Ogier I suppose

Otherwise, there's not really a universal list of teams, since the topic is pretty nuanced

Please be sure to check out our <#1304022827824844842>  section! Here are a few resources, tips, and walkthroughs to help you master the game:
## General Guide
- [Morimens New Players Handbook](<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1THjxR9hHs4NWHqSWHHa2dHAGuE_cRGelfzXdBfCnIlM/edit?usp=sharing>) by Cheri

- [Morimens Database and Teambuiler](https://skeydb.com/#/database) by Fjant
## Mode Specific
- [P-Dive Shortest Map Analysis](<https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1483756178340315216>) by Aurora
## Compendium
- [Morimens Compendium](<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1TCU7LJRzqKeuvLe97y_TfGZ-j6jq5bZX6hskAAJ2mFQ/edit?gid=167686509#gid=167686509>) by Mythag University

Since your Horla's so high her cards all get retain, right? So you could easily switch between fear poem on the scaling turns and anger on the burst turns (although switching a lot also means getting less Clem quasar triggers, and Clem wants as many as she can get. I usually try to switch only once to fear at the beginning and then once to anger at the end of the fight)

Imho, the best option would be dividing those characters in 3 different teams:
Clem/Horla/strength scaler dps in a team
Saya/Salvador/Thais/DPS in another team (i've seen you have GHelot, so maybe you could use her even if she doesn't benefit a lot from PCaro)
Mouchette/Aigis/Faint/Helot (with Lily replacing Faint when you get her) in another team

Bloodthirsty Flail isn't a derived card. They're not an exception.

Crimson Shackles isn't placed back into your deck when it transforms and it retains any orisons placed on the original copy of Crimson Shackles. If you shuffle Bloodthirsty Flail back into the deck, you'll draw it again.

Jenkin's card doesn't transform into Swarm Impact; it creates a copy of it and puts it back on top of the deck. It's very possible to draw into both Swarm Impact and the card that makes it in the same hand.

I mean, sure
Calling it an exception for simplicity sake
